rss.title: Thousands get meningitis vaccine as experts wait to see outbreak peak
rss.snippet: The outbreak, which has killed two people, is thought to have originated at a Canterbury nightclub.

rss.title: Five questions that still need answering about the meningitis outbreak
rss.snippet: The size and speed of the outbreak which has now affected 34 people, killing two of them, has been labelled "unprecedented".

rss.title: Lab-grown food pipe offers new hope for young patients
rss.snippet: UK scientists have grown fully functioning food pipes and successfully transplanted them into mini pigs, paving the way for human trials.

arxiv_papers.title: Global stability of a Hebbian/anti-Hebbian network for principal subspace learning
arxiv_papers.snippet: Biological neural networks self-organize according to local synaptic modifications to produce stable computations. How modifications at the synaptic level give rise to such computations at the network level remains an open question. Pehlevan et al. [Neur. Comp. 27 (2015), 1461--1495] proposed a mode

arxiv_papers.title: On graphical domination for threshold-linear networks with recurrent excitation and global inhibition
arxiv_papers.snippet: Graphical domination was first introduced in [1] in the context of combinatorial threshold-linear networks (CTLNs). There it was shown that when a domination relationship exists between a pair of vertices in a graph, certain fixed points in the corresponding CTLN can be ruled out. Here we prove two

podcast: [Science-Based Meditation Tools to Improve Your Brain & Health | Dr. Richard Davidson] Dr. Richard Davidson, PhD, is a professor of psychology and psychiatry at the University of Wisconsin–Madison and a pioneer in the scientific study of meditation. We discuss how meditation changes your brain and body, how just 5 minutes daily can improve focus, stress resilience and your overall health, and we cover different types of meditation.
